2. NYANLIN Inference Layer (No llama.cpp)
Weights တွေကို Matrix Multiplication လုပ်ဖို့အတွက် Native NumPy (သို့မဟုတ်) Pure Python logic ကို သုံးပါမယ်။ llama.cpp မပါတဲ့အတွက် Memory footprint အရမ်းနည်းသွားမှာပါ။

Python

လုပ်ဆောင်ချက် NYANLIN_INFERENCE(input_tokens, weights):
    # Hidden layers များကို တွက်ချက်ခြင်း
    x = weights['token_embeddings'][input_tokens]
    
    # Transformer Layer Logic (Pure MMC/Python)
    တစ်ခုချင်းစီ (for) i အားလုံး (in) အတိုင်းအတာ(num_layers):
        # Attention Layer (MMC Native Logic)
        attention_output = self.apply_attention(x, weights[f'layer_{i}_attention'])
        x = x + attention_output
        
        # Feed Forward Network
        ffn_output = self.apply_ffn(x, weights[f'layer_{i}_ffn'])
        x = x + ffn_output
        
    ပြန်ပေး self.softmax(x)
3. MMC Binary Tokenizer (Custom Logic)
NYANLIN အတွက် သီးသန့် Binary Tokenizer ကို MMC ရဲ့ smart_join logic ကို အသုံးပြုပြီး တည်ဆောက်ပါမယ်။ ဒါမှသာ Model က ထွက်လာတဲ့ binary token တွေကို မြန်မာစာအဖြစ် တိုက်ရိုက် ပြန်ပြောင်းနိုင်မှာပါ။

လက်ရှိကိုပြင်ထားတဲ့အရာ (gguf_parser.py)

- Header / magic constant ကိုသေချာစေပြီး auto-parse on init ထည့်ထားတယ်။

- metadata parsing, lazy-array skipping, alignment ပြဿနာများကို ပြင်ထားပြီး chunked read/seek logic ကို သေချာစေမယ်။

- tensor size / element count (n_total) အတွက် စစ်ဆေးပေးတယ် — multidimensional dims ကို support လုပ်တယ်။

- F16/F32 ဖတ်ခြင်းကို numpy.frombuffer(dtype='<f2' / '<f4') သုံးဖို့ ပြင်ထားတယ်။

- BF16 → float32 ပြောင်းလဲမှုကို safer bit-shift နည်းဖြင့် ပြင်ထားတယ် (uint16 << 16 -> view float32)။

- Q8_0, Q4_0, Q4_1 dequant functions ကို file/byte handling ချိုသာစေပြီး EOF စစ်ချက်၊ memoryview/struct.unpack_from သုံးမှုတို့နှင့်တကွ ပြင်ဆိုင်းထားတယ်။

- Q5_K (Q5_K_M) နှင့် Q8_1 လို ရှုပ်ထွေးတဲ့ quant formats များကို အခုသို့မဟုတ် placeholder (NotImplementedError) အဖြစ် ထားရှိထားတယ် — spec နဲ့ တိတိကျကျ implement လုပ်ဖို့ လိုတယ်။

- _type_size(), _type_name() helper functions ထည့်ထားတယ်။

ေကာင္းပါတယ္ — D ကိုေရြးလိုက္တာေၾကာင့္ NYANLIN အတြက္ mmc-compiler နဲ႔ “pure binary MMC” inference engine ကို ေဆာက္ဖို႔ အဆင့္လိုက္ ရွင္းျပခ်က္၊ လုပ္ရမယ့္အလုပ္ေတြ၊ လိုအပ္တဲ့ အစိတ္အပိုင္းေတြ၊ ရွာဖွႆရမ့ဲ သတင္းအခ်က္မ်ားကို မြန္မာဘာသာျဖင့္ ေဖာ္ျပေပးထားပါတယ္။



တိုႛခုကို အပိုင္း (phases) အလိုက္ ခြဲျပေပးထားျပီး၊ ဥပမာ တာဝန္စာရင္း၊ နည္းပညာ႐ွာေဖြမႈ၊ ရည္မွန္းခ်က္တိုမ်ား၊ အခ်ိန္ခန္႔မွန္းခ်က္၊ ရယူရမယ့္ sample ဖိုင္/ပံုစံ တို႔ ပါ၀င္ပါသည္။



အေျခခံသေဘာတရား (summary)

- ရည္မွန္းခ်က္ = mmc-compiler ကို အေျခခံ၍ llama.cpp ကို မသုံးပဲ NYANLIN “binary MMC” inference runtime (single native executable/library) တည္ေဆာက္ျခင္း။

- အဓိက လုပ္ငန္း = (1) mmc IR / model-format ဖန္တီး => (2) transpiler/packager သို႔ေတာ့ binary model (.mmc) ထုတ္ => (3) native runtime ရေရးသားျခင္း (C/C++ သို႔ Rust) => (4) tokenizer integration + KV-cache + generation loop => (5) tests & optimizations => (6) packaging/distribution



Phase 0 — စီမံခ်က္ႏွင့္ စမ္းသပ္ေျပာင္းလဲျခင္း (Planning & Analysis) (1-2 ရက္)

- ရည္ညႊန္း: တည္ေဆာက္မယ့္ ပလက္ျဖ့မ္ (x86_64 Linux, Windows, macOS, ARM) နဲ့ ေက်းဇူးျပဳမကြာမွ (CPU-only vs GPU) သတ္မွတ္။

- စမ္းေသာဝိုင္လ်: mmc-compiler repo ကို ျပည့္ဝၿပိးေသခ်ာလုပ္၍ ဖိုင္အသြင္ (tokenizer, gguf parser, transpiler) ကိုလေ့လာ။

- သတ္မွတ္: supported quant formats (Q4_0, Q4_1, Q5_K, Q8_0, BF16, F16, F32) — အခု implement လုပ္မယ့္ formats ကို သတ္မွတ္။

- ရယူရမယ့္ sample model(s): အသင့္တင္ထားေသာ GGUF model မ်ား (သို႔မဟုတ္ mmc-converted) — unit tests ႏွင့် validation ကို run မယ္။



Phase 1 — Model Format & Packaging (2–7 ရက္)

- ေသခ်ာစရန္: သက္ဆိုင္ရာ model-format တစ္ခု ဆံုးျဖတ္ (options)

  - Option A: GGUF-compatible binary (reuse GGUF metadata & tensor layout) — ကောင်းတဲ့ compatibility

  - Option B: own MMC-binary format (.mmc) optimized for fast mmap & native layout (preferred for performance)

- ေဆာက္ရမယ့္အရာ:

  - Model packer: Python/Go tool that takes GGUF or raw tensors -> packs into single binary (.mmc) with header, index, and aligned tensor blobs. Include simple metadata (vocab, merges, tokenizer type, dims, quant type).

  - Choose tensor layout: contiguous row-major, aligned to 32/64 bytes, store quant format per tensor + offset table.

  - Provide versioning & magic bytes, checksums.

- Deliverables: packer CLI, format spec docs, example .mmc



Phase 2 — Transpiler / Compiler Integration (mmc-compiler) (3–10 ရက္)

- ကိရိယာ: mmc-compiler ကိုသုံး၍ model->runtime နဲ့ codegen ပိုင္းကို သတ္မွတ္။

- လုပ္ရမယ့္အရာ:

  - mmc-compiler ကို modularize: frontend (parse tokenizer + model metadata) + backend (emit C/Rust runtime glue or binary pack).

  - Implement an IR (intermediate representation) simple enough to express layers (matmul, add, rmsnorm, gelu, attention).

  - Implement transpiler target that emits:

    - metadata (.h or JSON)

    - precomputed constants

    - optionally native code for small kernels (biases, scales) for speed

  - Option: emit a serialized schedule (operators + parameters) for runtime to execute (no generated C code necessary).

- Deliverables: integration PR in mmc-compiler, example transpilation run that produces .mmc + runtime metadata.



Phase 3 — Native Runtime (Core) (2–6 weeks depending on scope)

- ဘာဘာသာစကားရွေးချယ္: Rust (safety + modern toolchain) သို႔မဟုတ္ C++ (existing libs & easy to bind) — recommendation: Rust for new project, C++ if expecting many external libs.

- အဓိကလိုအပ္ခ်က္:

  - Model loader: mmap() or read(), parse header, build tensor index, lazy load large tensors

  - Memory layout: align tensors to cache lines, store quantized bytes for direct kernel consumption

  - Dequantizers for supported formats implemented in native code (vectorized):

    - Q8_0: load 2-byte f16 scale + 32 int8 -> multiply with scale (vectorized)

    - Q4_0/Q4_1: nibble unpack + scale/min -> vectorize using SIMD

    - Q5_K: implement carefully per spec (bitpacking, super-block)

  - Matrix multiplication engine:

    - Implement optimized CPU-only GEMM for quantized formats (int8 / packed-nibble -> float accum)

    - Use blocked (tiled) matmul, threadpool, and SIMD intrinsics (AVX2/AVX512 for x86, NEON for ARM)

    - Option: integrate BLIS/OpenBLAS for float kernels, and write custom quant kernels for quantized mats

  - Attention and transformer ops: implement fused kernels when possible: QKV projection, attention softmax (with numerical stability), rotary/ALiBi, feed-forward (GELU/SwiGLU).

  - KV-cache (for generation): efficient append, reuse memory, support beam/sample.

  - Tokenizer bridge: expose tokenizer (Python or native) — prefer keep tokenizer in Python but provide C FFI for speed; optionally build tokenizer into binary (SentencePiece or custom).

  - Multi-threading: threadpool, control concurrency, set OMP env optional.

  - API: C ABI + small CLI for interactive generation, or CLI only.

- Deliverables: native runtime repo/module, unit tests for kernels, example CLI that loads .mmc and runs a prompt.



Phase 4 — Tokenizer & Vocabulary (1–3 days)

- Implement tokenizer in native or use existing libs (sentencepiece, tokenizers from HuggingFace compiled via Rust).

- Store tokenizer config inside .mmc (vocab, merges, special tokens) for self-contained binary.

- Provide bindings so runtime can tokenize input & detokenize outputs.



Phase 5 — Generation Loop & Sampling (2–5 days)

- Implement generation strategies: greedy, top-k, top-p, temperature, typical sampling, beam search (optional).

- Implement logits biasing, repetition penalty, stop tokens, streaming output.

- Integration with KV-cache for autoregressive speed.



Phase 6 — Testing, Validation & Benchmarks (1–2 weeks)

- Unit tests:

  - Compare output of native runtime with reference Python inference (NYANLIN original) on small inputs for correctness.

  - Test dequantizers with known arrays to ensure bit-exact conversion.

- Performance benchmarks:

  - latency / throughput for seq lengths, batch sizes, different quant formats.

  - memory usage (resident) for large models.

- Regression: numerical validation across platforms.



Phase 7 — Packaging, Cross-compile & Distribution (3–7 days)

- Build single static executable per platform (musl for Linux if desired).

- Provide pip wheel (if exposing Python bindings), or release binaries via GitHub Releases.

- Docker images for CPU-only and GPU (if implemented).

- CI: GitHub Actions to build/test on Linux/macOS/Windows.



Phase 8 — Optimization & Advanced Features (ongoing)

- Implement AVX512 kernels, tensor core CUDA / ROCm / Metal backends if GPU support needed.

- Memory-mapped streaming for extremely large models (load tiles on demand).

- Quant-aware matmul lowering (avoid dequantizing whole tensors; multiply quantized bytes with scales).

- Fused kernels (gemm+add+act) for speed & lower memory traffic.

- Support 4-bit matrix multiplication directly (avoid expanding to int8 every time).



Minimum viable feature set (MVP)

- Model packer (GGUF -> .mmc)

- Native runtime (CPU-only) that supports F32, F16, BF16, Q8_0, Q4_0, Q4_1 dequant

- Working tokenizer (SentencePiece / HF tokenizers)

- CLI that performs autoregressive generation with KV-cache

- Unit tests and a small example model and prompt



Concrete Technical Recommendations

- Language: Rust (safety, excellent FFI, cargo, cross-compile). If team prefers C/C++ for ecosystem, choose C++17.

- SIMD: use portable abstraction (e.g., packed_simd / std::simd in Rust) or intrinsics per arch.

- Threading: Rayon (Rust) or custom threadpool; avoid global OpenMP surprises.

- IO: use mmap on Linux/macOS for fast load; fallback to buffered read on Windows.

- Build system: cargo (Rust) / CMake (C++).

- Testing: pytest harness that compares Python vs native outputs for small models.

- Benchmark harness: simple script to measure tokens-per-second (single-thread & multi-thread).



Risks & Challenges

- Correct implementation of complex quant formats (Q5_K, Q8_1) requires spec or test models. Wrong implementation => silent bad outputs.

- Performance tuning (SIMD kernels) is time-consuming and platform-specific.

- Numerical differences between float16/bfloat16 and float32: ensure deterministic ops for tests.

- Legal: model licensing and distribution rights. Do not redistribute proprietary GGUF models.

- Security: avoid running untrusted model code; sandbox CLI.



Example task breakdown (first 4 sprints)

- Sprint 0 (analysis): finalize target platforms & quant formats; collect sample GGUF models. (3 days)

- Sprint 1 (format + packer): implement .mmc packer + spec + simple loader in Python. (5 days)

- Sprint 2 (runtime core MVP): implement native loader, F32/F16/BF16 + Q8_0 + Q4_0 dequant + basic matmul (non-optimized). CLI to run model. (10–14 days)

- Sprint 3 (optimizations + tokenizer): add tokenizer integration, KV-cache, multi-threading, basic SIMD optimizations; tests + benchmarks. (14 days)

- Sprint 4 (hard quant & packaging): implement Q4_1, Q5_K (if spec available), packaging, cross-compile, CI. (2–4 weeks)

၁။ .mmc Binary Format Specification (v1.0)
ဒီ format ကို performance မြှင့်ဖို့အတွက် Alignment ကို အဓိကထားပါတယ်။ Memory-map (mmap) လုပ်တဲ့အခါ CPU က tensor တွေကို တိုက်ရိုက်ဖတ်နိုင်အောင် bytes တွေကို ၆၄-ဘစ် အလိုက် စီစဉ်ထားပါတယ်။

Section	Size (Bytes)	Description
Magic Number	4	0x4D 0x4D 0x43 0x21 (ASCII: "MMC!")
Version	4	0x00000001 (Uint32)
Meta Size	8	JSON Metadata ရဲ့ အရှည် (Uint64)
Metadata	Variable	Tokenizer vocab, Model architecture (JSON format)
Alignment	Variable	Metadata အပြီးမှာ Tensor တွေ 64-byte aligned ဖြစ်အောင် pad လုပ်ခြင်း
Tensor Index	Variable	တစ်ခုချင်းစီရဲ့ offset, size, shape နှင့် type (Binary table)
Tensor Data	Variable	အမှန်တကယ် quantized weights များ (Raw bytes)

ဒီ script က GGUF ဖိုင်ကနေ Master ရဲ့ .mmc binary ဖိုင်ကို Transpile (Convert) လုပ်ပေးမှာ ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

Python

import json
import struct
import numpy as np

class MMCPacker:
    def __init__(self, output_path):
        self.output_path = output_path
        self.magic = b"MMC!"
        self.version = 1
        
    def pack(self, metadata, tensors):
        with open(self.output_path, "wb") as f:
            # 1. Header ရေးခြင်း
            meta_json = json.dumps(metadata).encode('utf-8')
            f.write(self.magic)
            f.write(struct.pack("<I", self.version))
            f.write(struct.pack("<Q", len(meta_json)))
            f.write(meta_json)
            
            # 2. Padding for 64-byte alignment
            current_pos = f.tell()
            padding = (64 - (current_pos % 64)) % 64
            f.write(b'\x00' * padding)
            
            # 3. Tensor Data (Placeholder logic for MVP)
            # Master ရဲ့ gguf_parser ကနေရလာတဲ့ tensors တွေကို ဒီမှာ loop ပတ်ပြီးသိမ်းမယ်
            for name, data in tensors.items():
                f.write(data.tobytes())
        print(f"Successfully packed: {self.output_path}")

# Usage Example:
# packer = MMCPacker("nyanlin_core.mmc")
# packer.pack({"model": "nyanlin-lite", "vocab_size": 32000}, {"weight1": np.array([1,2,3])})
၃။ Basic Runtime Skeleton (C++)
ဒါကတော့ .mmc ဖိုင်ကို Memory-map သုံးပြီး အမြန်ဆုံး ဖတ်ပြနိုင်မယ့် C++ အခြေခံ Structure ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

C++

#include <iostream>
#include <fcntl.h>
#include <sys/mman.h>
#include <sys/stat.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#include <vector>

struct MMCHeader {
    char magic[4];
    uint32_t version;
    uint64_t meta_size;
};

class MMCRuntime {
private:
    void* mmap_ptr;
    size_t file_size;

public:
    bool load_model(const std::string& path) {
        int fd = open(path.c_str(), O_RDONLY);
        if (fd == -1) return false;

        struct stat st;
        fstat(fd, &st);
        file_size = st.st_size;

        // mmap သုံးပြီး file ကို memory ထဲ တိုက်ရိုက်ချိတ်ဆက်ခြင်း
        mmap_ptr = mmap(NULL, file_size, PROT_READ, MAP_PRIVATE, fd, 0);
        close(fd);

        if (mmap_ptr == MAP_FAILED) return false;

        MMCHeader* header = (MMCHeader*)mmap_ptr;
        if (std::string(header->magic, 4) != "MMC!") {
            std::cerr << "Invalid MMC Format!" << std::endl;
            return false;
        }

        std::cout << "NYANLIN Core Loaded. Version: " << header->version << std::endl;
        return true;
    }

    ~MMCRuntime() {
        if (mmap_ptr) munmap(mmap_ptr, file_size);
    }
};

int main() {
    MMCRuntime nyanlin;
    if (nyanlin.load_model("nyanlin_core.mmc")) {
        std::cout << "Ready for Inference." << std::endl;
    }
    return 0;
}

````markdown name=MMC_SPEC.md

# MMC Format (prototype) — .mmc



Version: 0.1 (prototype)

Endianness: little-endian



High-level goals:

- Single-file, memory-map-friendly binary for fast native runtime loading.

- Simple header, JSON metadata blob, tensor index, then contiguous tensor blobs.

- Alignment friendly (64-byte alignment for header/sections) for CPU caches.



Layout (file byte offsets, all offsets are from file start):



0x00: Header (fixed 64 bytes)

  - magic: 4 bytes ASCII "MMCF" (0x4D 0x4D 0x43 0x46)

  - version: u32 (little-endian) -> currently 1

  - metadata_size: u64 -> size in bytes of the metadata JSON blob

  - n_tensors: u32 -> number of tensors in index

  - index_offset: u64 -> offset (from file start) to the beginning of the tensor index

  - reserved/padding: remaining bytes up to 64 bytes total



After header:

  - metadata_blob (JSON utf-8) length = metadata_size bytes

    - The JSON contains model-level metadata (tokenizer type, vocab size, model name, optional original GGUF metadata keys).

    - The runtime can parse JSON to obtain tokenizer config, special tokens, other info.

  - metadata padded to 64-byte boundary



At index_offset:

  - tensor_index (variable length): sequential entries; entries themselves are unaligned but entire index begins at index_offset which is aligned.

  - Each tensor index entry:

      - name_len: u32

      - name: name_len bytes (utf-8)

      - n_dims: u32

      - dims: n_dims × u64 (each dim as u64 little-endian)

      - elem_type: u32 (see Element types below)

      - data_offset: u64 (offset from file start to tensor blob)

      - data_size: u64 (byte size of tensor blob)

  - There are n_tensors entries.



After index:

  - tensor blobs — raw bytes for each tensor (in order). The runtime uses elem_type and data_size to interpret.



Element types (elem_type values)

  - 0 = F32 (float32)

  - 1 = F16 (IEEE 754 float16)

  - 2 = BF16 (bfloat16, stored as 16-bit)

  - 3 = Q8_0 (block quantized; store raw bytes as in GGUF Q8_0 layout)

  - 4 = Q4_0

  - 5 = Q4_1

  - 6 = RAW_UINT8 (for vocab arrays, etc.)

  - (Extend as needed; for complex formats store raw blob and implement dequant in runtime)



Notes / Guarantees

  - All multi-byte integers and floats are little-endian.

  - The metadata blob is JSON for easy extensibility.

  - Tensors are packed as contiguous blobs so runtime can mmap and point into the mapped area.

  - data_offset should be aligned to 64 bytes for improved performance.

  - data_size is exact number of bytes for that tensor blob.

  - The tensor index should fit into memory; for extremely large numbers of small tensors you may chunk or compress the index.



Rationale & usage

  - The runtime mmc_reader will mmap file, read header & metadata JSON, then read tensor index and map pointers into the mmap region for each tensor (no copying).

  - For quantized tensors (Q4/Q8/Q5), keep the raw packed representation in the blob and implement dequant kernels that operate on the packed bytes to avoid expanding them unnecessarily.



Example minimal header values (pseudo):

  magic = b"MMCF"

  version = 1

  metadata_size = 1024

  n_tensors = 42

  index_offset = 64 + pad_to_64(metadata_size)



Design note: This prototype format trades simplicity for features. If you need backward compatibility with GGUF, packers can store the original GGUF metadata as a JSON field and place raw tensor contents as blobs (extracted from GGUF) so the .mmc remains a simple container optimized for native loading.

````



```python name=mmc_packer.py

#!/usr/bin/env python3

"""

mmc_packer.py - prototype packer: GGUF v3  -> .mmc (prototype)



Usage:

    python mmc_packer.py input.gguf output.mmc



Notes:

- This is a prototype. It reads a GGUF v3 file (basic types and tensor index),

  extracts metadata and tensor blobs, writes a compact .mmc format (see MMC_SPEC.md).

- For safety and simplicity the packer fully reads GGUF metadata (could be large).

- The packer stores metadata in JSON (utf-8). Tensor blob bytes are copied unchanged.

"""



import struct

import json

import sys

from functools import reduce

from operator import mul



MAGIC_GGUF = 0x46554747  # "GGUF" little-endian

MMC_MAGIC = b"MMCF"

MMC_VERSION = 1

HEADER_SIZE = 64

ALIGN = 64



# GGUF value types (subset)

GGUF_TYPE_UINT8 = 0

GGUF_TYPE_INT8 = 1

GGUF_TYPE_UINT16 = 2

GGUF_TYPE_INT16 = 3

GGUF_TYPE_UINT32 = 4

GGUF_TYPE_INT32 = 5

GGUF_TYPE_FLOAT32 = 6

GGUF_TYPE_BOOL = 7

GGUF_TYPE_STRING = 8

GGUF_TYPE_ARRAY = 9

GGUF_TYPE_UINT64 = 10

GGUF_TYPE_INT64 = 11

GGUF_TYPE_FLOAT64 = 12



# GGML tensor types (subset mapped into .mmc elem types)

GGML_TYPE_F32 = 0

GGML_TYPE_F16 = 1

GGML_TYPE_BF16 = 2

GGML_TYPE_Q8_0 = 3

GGML_TYPE_Q4_0 = 4

GGML_TYPE_Q4_1 = 5

GGML_TYPE_RAW_UINT8 = 6



def align_up(x, a=ALIGN):

    return ((x + a - 1) // a) * a



def read_gguf_header(f):

    magic = struct.unpack("<I", f.read(4))[0]

    if magic != MAGIC_GGUF:

        raise ValueError("Not a GGUF v3 file (bad magic)")

    version = struct.unpack("<I", f.read(4))[0]

    n_tensors = struct.unpack("<Q", f.read(8))[0]

    n_kv = struct.unpack("<Q", f.read(8))[0]

    return version, n_tensors, n_kv



def read_value(f, dtype):

    if dtype == GGUF_TYPE_UINT8:

        return struct.unpack("<B", f.read(1))[0]

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_INT8:

        return struct.unpack("<b", f.read(1))[0]

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_UINT16:

        return struct.unpack("<H", f.read(2))[0]

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_INT16:

        return struct.unpack("<h", f.read(2))[0]

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_UINT32:

        return struct.unpack("<I", f.read(4))[0]

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_INT32:

        return struct.unpack("<i", f.read(4))[0]

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_UINT64:

        return struct.unpack("<Q", f.read(8))[0]

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_INT64:

        return struct.unpack("<q", f.read(8))[0]

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_FLOAT32:

        return struct.unpack("<f", f.read(4))[0]

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_FLOAT64:

        return struct.unpack("<d", f.read(8))[0]

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_BOOL:

        return struct.unpack("<?", f.read(1))[0]

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_STRING:

        length = struct.unpack("<Q", f.read(8))[0]

        return f.read(length).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")

    elif dtype == GGUF_TYPE_ARRAY:

        elem_type = struct.unpack("<I", f.read(4))[0]

        count = struct.unpack("<Q", f.read(8))[0]

        res = []

        for _ in range(count):

            res.append(read_value(f, elem_type))

        return res

    else:

        raise ValueError("Unknown GGUF value type: %s" % dtype)



def parse_metadata(f, n_kv):

    km = {}

    for _ in range(n_kv):

        key_len_bytes = f.read(8)

        if not key_len_bytes:

            break

        key_len = struct.unpack("<Q", key_len_bytes)[0]

        key = f.read(key_len).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")



        val_type = struct.unpack("<I", f.read(4))[0]

        if val_type == GGUF_TYPE_ARRAY:

            elem_type = struct.unpack("<I", f.read(4))[0]

            count = struct.unpack("<Q", f.read(8))[0]

            # Read array contents (naive)

            arr = []

            for _ in range(count):

                arr.append(read_value(f, elem_type))

            km[key] = arr

        else:

            km[key] = read_value(f, val_type)

    # align 32 bytes (GGUF) before tensor infos

    pos = f.tell()

    aligned = ((pos + 31) // 32) * 32

    if aligned > pos:

        f.seek(aligned)

    return km



def parse_tensor_infos(f, n_tensors):

    infos = []

    for _ in range(n_tensors):

        name_len = struct.unpack("<Q", f.read(8))[0]

        if name_len > 10000:

            raise ValueError("Suspicious tensor name length")

        name = f.read(name_len).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")



        n_dims = struct.unpack("<I", f.read(4))[0]

        dims = []

        for _ in range(n_dims):

            dims.append(struct.unpack("<Q", f.read(8))[0])



        tensor_type = struct.unpack("<I", f.read(4))[0]

        offset = struct.unpack("<Q", f.read(8))[0]

        infos.append({

            "name": name,

            "n_dims": n_dims,

            "dims": dims,

            "type": tensor_type,

            "offset": offset

        })

    # align 32 bytes

    pos = f.tell()

    aligned = ((pos + 31) // 32) * 32

    if aligned > pos:

        f.seek(aligned)

    data_start = f.tell()

    return infos, data_start



def gguf_type_size(elem_type, dims):

    # dims: list -> product

    n_total = 1 if len(dims) == 0 else reduce(mul, dims, 1)

    if elem_type == GGML_TYPE_F32:

        return n_total * 4

    elif elem_type == GGML_TYPE_F16 or elem_type == GGML_TYPE_BF16:

        return n_total * 2

    elif elem_type == GGML_TYPE_Q8_0:

        QK = 32

        block_size = 2 + QK

        n_blocks = (n_total + QK - 1) // QK

        return n_blocks * block_size

    elif elem_type == GGML_TYPE_Q4_0:

        QK = 32

        block_size = 2 + QK // 2

        n_blocks = (n_total + QK - 1) // QK

        return n_blocks * block_size

    elif elem_type == GGML_TYPE_Q4_1:

        QK = 32

        block_size = 2 + 2 + QK // 2

        n_blocks = (n_total + QK - 1) // QK

        return n_blocks * block_size

    else:

        # fallback: unknown format can't be sized; raise

        raise NotImplementedError("Type size not implemented for elem_type %d" % elem_type)



def convert_gguf_to_mmc(gguf_path, mmc_path):

    with open(gguf_path, "rb") as f:

        version, n_tensors, n_kv = read_gguf_header(f)

        print("GGUF version", version, "tensors", n_tensors, "kv", n_kv)

        metadata = parse_metadata(f, n_kv)

        tensor_infos, data_start = parse_tensor_infos(f, n_tensors)



        # read tensor blobs (from data_start + offset)

        for ti in tensor_infos:

            elem_type = ti["type"]

            dims = ti["dims"]

            size = gguf_type_size(elem_type, dims)

            f.seek(data_start + ti["offset"])

            blob = f.read(size)

            ti["byte_size"] = len(blob)

            ti["_blob"] = blob



    # Prepare .mmc metadata JSON

    mmc_metadata = {

        "source": "gguf",

        "gguf_version": version,

        "gguf_metadata": metadata,

        "n_tensors": len(tensor_infos)

    }

    metadata_bytes = json.dumps(mmc_metadata, ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")

    metadata_size = len(metadata_bytes)



    # compute index and offsets

    index_entries = []

    # we will write header (64), metadata (aligned), then index, then tensor blobs

    metadata_offset = HEADER_SIZE

    metadata_pad = align_up(metadata_size) - metadata_size

    index_offset = metadata_offset + metadata_size + metadata_pad



    # compute where blobs will start: index size depends on names/dims; generate index entries first with placeholders

    # We'll build index in memory, compute its size, then fix data offsets

    index_bytes = bytearray()

    for ti in tensor_infos:

        name_b = ti["name"].encode("utf-8")

        index_bytes += struct.pack("<I", len(name_b))

        index_bytes += name_b

        index_bytes += struct.pack("<I", ti["n_dims"])

        for d in ti["dims"]:

            index_bytes += struct.pack("<Q", d)

        # map gguf tensor type to mmc elem_type (we reuse the GGML type numeric values)

        index_bytes += struct.pack("<I", ti["type"])

        # placeholders for offset and size (will fill later)

        index_bytes += struct.pack("<Q", 0)  # data_offset placeholder

        index_bytes += struct.pack("<Q", ti["byte_size"])

    index_size = len(index_bytes)

    index_pad = 0  # we don't require extra pad for the index itself; data start will be aligned

    data_start = index_offset + index_size + index_pad

    data_start = align_up(data_start)



    # fill data offsets

    cur = data_start

    final_blobs = []

    idx_cursor = 0

    out_index = bytearray()

    for ti in tensor_infos:

        name_b = ti["name"].encode("utf-8")

        out_index += struct.pack("<I", len(name_b))

        out_index += name_b

        out_index += struct.pack("<I", ti["n_dims"])

        for d in ti["dims"]:

            out_index += struct.pack("<Q", d)

        out_index += struct.pack("<I", ti["type"])

        out_index += struct.pack("<Q", cur)

        out_index += struct.pack("<Q", ti["byte_size"])

        final_blobs.append(ti["_blob"])

        cur += ti["byte_size"]

        # align each blob start to 64 bytes for performance

        cur = align_up(cur)



    # Write .mmc

    with open(mmc_path, "wb") as out:

        # header

        header = bytearray(HEADER_SIZE)

        header[0:4] = MMC_MAGIC

        header[4:8] = struct.pack("<I", MMC_VERSION)

        header[8:16] = struct.pack("<Q", metadata_size)

        header[16:20] = struct.pack("<I", len(tensor_infos))

        header[20:28] = struct.pack("<Q", index_offset)

        # rest reserved / zero

        out.write(header)



        # metadata

        out.write(metadata_bytes)

        if metadata_pad:

            out.write(b"\x00" * metadata_pad)



        # index

        out.write(out_index)

        # pad up to aligned data_start

        cur_pos = out.tell()

        if cur_pos < data_start:

            out.write(b"\x00" * (data_start - cur_pos))



        # tensor blobs

        for blob in final_blobs:

            out.write(blob)

            # pad to 64-byte for next blob

            pos = out.tell()

            pad = align_up(pos) - pos

            if pad:

                out.write(b"\x00" * pad)



    print("Wrote .mmc:", mmc_path, "metadata_size:", metadata_size, "n_tensors:", len(tensor_infos))





if __name__ == "__main__":

    if len(sys.argv) != 3:

        print("Usage: python mmc_packer.py input.gguf output.mmc")

        sys.exit(1)

    convert_gguf_to_mmc(sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2])

```cpp name=mmc_reader.cpp

// Simple mmc_reader prototype (POSIX) - memory-maps .mmc and lists header, metadata, and tensors.

// Build (Linux/macOS): g++ -std=c++17 mmc_reader.cpp -O2 -o mmc_reader

// Run: ./mmc_reader model.mmc



#include <sys/mman.h>

#include <sys/stat.h>

#include <fcntl.h>

#include <unistd.h>



#include <cstdint>

#include <cstring>

#include <iostream>

#include <string>

#include <vector>

#include <stdexcept>



struct MmcHeader {

    char magic[4];   // "MMCF"

    uint32_t version;

    uint64_t metadata_size;

    uint32_t n_tensors;

    uint64_t index_offset;

    // total header = 64 bytes; rest is reserved

};



static inline size_t align_up(size_t x, size_t a=64) {

    return ((x + a - 1) / a) * a;

}



int main(int argc, char** argv) {

    if (argc != 2) {

        std::cerr << "Usage: " << argv[0] << " model.mmc\n";

        return 1;

    }

    const char* path = argv[1];



    int fd = open(path, O_RDONLY);

    if (fd < 0) {

        perror("open");

        return 1;

    }



    struct stat st;

    if (fstat(fd, &st) != 0) {

        perror("fstat");

        close(fd);

        return 1;

    }

    size_t size = st.st_size;

    if (size < sizeof(MmcHeader)) {

        std::cerr << "File too small\n";

        close(fd);

        return 1;

    }



    void* p = mmap(nullptr, size, PROT_READ, MAP_PRIVATE, fd, 0);

    if (p == MAP_FAILED) {

        perror("mmap");

        close(fd);

        return 1;

    }



    uint8_t* base = reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(p);



    MmcHeader header;

    memcpy(&header, base, sizeof(MmcHeader));

    if (std::memcmp(header.magic, "MMCF", 4) != 0) {

        std::cerr << "Bad MMC magic\n";

        munmap(p, size);

        close(fd);

        return 1;

    }

    std::cout << "MMC version " << header.version << ", metadata_size="

              << header.metadata_size << ", n_tensors=" << header.n_tensors

              << ", index_offset=" << header.index_offset << "\n";



    // read metadata JSON blob (not parsing JSON here)

    if (header.metadata_size > 0) {

        size_t meta_off = sizeof(MmcHeader);

        if (meta_off + header.metadata_size > size) {

            std::cerr << "Metadata out of range\n";

            munmap(p, size);

            close(fd);

            return 1;

        }

        std::string meta((char*)(base + meta_off), header.metadata_size);

        std::cout << "Metadata (first 512 chars):\n";

        std::cout << meta.substr(0, 512) << (meta.size() > 512 ? "...\n" : "\n");

    }



    // walk the index

    size_t idx = header.index_offset;

    for (uint32_t ti = 0; ti < header.n_tensors; ++ti) {

        if (idx + 4 > size) { std::cerr << "Index truncated\n"; break; }

        uint32_t name_len = *reinterpret_cast<uint32_t*>(base + idx);

        idx += 4;

        if (idx + name_len > size) { std::cerr << "Index name truncated\n"; break; }

        std::string name((char*)(base + idx), name_len);

        idx += name_len;

        if (idx + 4 > size) { std::cerr << "Index truncated (n_dims)\n"; break; }

        uint32_t n_dims = *reinterpret_cast<uint32_t*>(base + idx);

        idx += 4;

        std::vector<uint64_t> dims;

        dims.reserve(n_dims);

        for (uint32_t d = 0; d < n_dims; ++d) {

            if (idx + 8 > size) { std::cerr << "Index dims truncated\n"; break; }

            uint64_t dv = *reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(base + idx);

            idx += 8;

            dims.push_back(dv);

        }

        if (idx + 4 + 8 + 8 > size) { std::cerr << "Index entry truncated\n"; break; }

        uint32_t elem_type = *reinterpret_cast<uint32_t*>(base + idx);

        idx += 4;

        uint64_t data_offset = *reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(base + idx);

        idx += 8;

        uint64_t data_size = *reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(base + idx);

        idx += 8;



        std::cout << "Tensor[" << ti << "]: name='" << name << "' type=" << elem_type

                  << " dims=[";

        for (size_t i=0;i<dims.size();++i){ if (i) std::cout << ","; std::cout << dims[i]; }

        std::cout << "] data_offset=" << data_offset << " data_size=" << data_size << "\n";



        // Example: create pointer to tensor bytes inside mmap (no copy)

        if (data_offset + data_size <= size) {

            const uint8_t* tptr = base + data_offset;

            // For debugging, e.g., print first bytes

            size_t show = std::min<size_t>(16, data_size);

            std::cout << "  data[0.." << show << "]:";

            for (size_t b = 0; b < show; ++b) {

                printf(" %02x", tptr[b]);

            }

            std::cout << "\n";

        } else {

            std::cout << "  -> data out of file bounds\n";

        }

    }



    munmap(p, size);

    close(fd);

    return 0;

}

၁။ Q8_0 Dequantization (AVX2 Optimization)
Q8_0 သည် 32-block size ရှိပြီး f16 scale တစ်ခုနှင့် int8 weights ၃၂ ခု ပါဝင်ပါတယ်။ AVX2 သုံးပြီး တစ်ကြိမ်တည်းနဲ့ float32 ၈ ခုကို ပြိုင်တူထုတ်ပေးပါမယ်။

C++

#include <immintrin.h>

// Q8_0 Block Structure
struct block_q8_0 {
    uint16_t d;       // delta (f16 format)
    int8_t  qs[32];   // weights
};

// Dequantize logic using AVX2
void dequantize_q8_0_avx2(const block_q8_0* blocks, float* output, int n_blocks) {
    for (int i = 0; i < n_blocks; ++i) {
        // f16 scale ကို float32 ပြောင်းခြင်း
        float d = _cvtsh_ss(blocks[i].d); 
        __m256 v_d = _mm256_set1_ps(d);

        for (int j = 0; j < 32; j += 8) {
            // int8 weight ၈ ခုကို load လုပ်ပြီး int32 ပြောင်းခြင်း
            __m128i v_qs8 = _mm_loadu_si128((const __m128i*)(blocks[i].qs + j));
            __m256i v_qs32 = _mm256_cvtepi8_epi32(v_qs8);

            // float32 သို့ပြောင်းပြီး scale နှင့် မြှောက်ခြင်း
            __m256 v_f32 = _mm256_cvtepi32_ps(v_qs32);
            v_f32 = _mm256_mul_ps(v_f32, v_d);

            // Output array ထဲသို့ သိမ်းခြင်း
            _mm256_storeu_ps(output + i * 32 + j, v_f32);
        }
    }
}
၂။ Q4_0 Dequantization (SIMD Unpacking)
Q4_0 သည် 4-bit weights များကို သုံးသောကြောင့် memory အရမ်းသက်သာသော်လည်း unpacking လုပ်ရတာ ပိုရှုပ်ထွေးပါတယ်။ အောက်ပါ logic က nibbles (4-bit) တွေကို ခွဲထုတ်ပြီး scale လုပ်ပေးပါတယ်။

C++

struct block_q4_0 {
    uint16_t d;       // delta (f16)
    uint8_t  qs[16];  // 32 x 4-bit weights
};

void dequantize_q4_0_avx2(const block_q4_0* blocks, float* output, int n_blocks) {
    __m256i low_mask = _mm256_set1_epi8(0x0F); // Lower 4 bits mask

    for (int i = 0; i < n_blocks; ++i) {
        float d = _cvtsh_ss(blocks[i].d);
        __m256 v_d = _mm256_set1_ps(d);

        // Load 16 bytes (32 weights)
        __m128i raw = _mm_loadu_si128((const __m128i*)blocks[i].qs);
        
        // Lower Nibbles (0-15)
        __m128i lo = _mm_and_si128(raw, _mm_set1_epi8(0x0F));
        // Upper Nibbles (16-31)
        __m128i hi = _mm_and_si128(_mm_srli_epi16(raw, 4), _mm_set1_epi8(0x0F));

        // Note: 4-bit weights များကို integer offset ပြန်နှုတ်ရန် လိုအပ်နိုင်သည် (GGML logic အတိုင်း)
        // ဤနေရာတွင် ရိုးရှင်းစေရန် scale မြှောက်ရုံသာ ပြထားပါသည်
        // ... (ဒါက Master ရဲ့ model quantization spec ပေါ်မူတည်ပါတယ်)
    }
}

// dequant_avx2_q8_q4.cpp

//

// AVX2 implementations (with scalar fallbacks) for GGUF-style Q8_0 and Q4_0 blocks.

//

// Build: g++ -O3 -mavx2 -mf16c -msse4.1 dequant_avx2_q8_q4.cpp -o dequant_test

//

// Notes:

//  - Assumes little-endian layout: each block begins with 2-byte float16 (IEEE 754 half) scale,

//    followed by 32 int8 values (Q8_0) OR 16 packed bytes (Q4_0).

//  - Input `src` points to contiguous blocks (no header). Functions accept n_elements (total

//    number of output elements) to handle trailing partial blocks.

//  - Functions write n_elements floats into dst[] (caller allocated).

//  - Requires AVX2 & SSE4.1 for the optimized path; scalar fallback used if not enabled.



#include <immintrin.h>

#include <cstdint>

#include <cstddef>

#include <cstring>

#include <cassert>

#include <cmath>



// ---------- helper: half -> float (portable scalar) ----------

// Convert IEEE-754 binary16 (uint16_t) to float (32-bit).

// This implementation is branchless and reasonably fast for scalar conversion.

// Reference idea: convert to float32 by reconstructing exponent/mantissa.

static inline float half_to_float(uint16_t h) {

    // Based on well-known bit-twiddling conversion.

    uint32_t sign = (h & 0x8000u) << 16;

    uint32_t exp  = (h & 0x7C00u);

    uint32_t mant = (h & 0x03FFu);



    uint32_t f;

    if (exp == 0x0000u) {

        if (mant == 0) {

            // zero

            f = sign;

        } else {

            // subnormal -> normalize

            // shift mantissa until leading 1

            int shift = 0;

            while ((mant & 0x0400u) == 0) {

                mant <<= 1;

                shift++;

            }

            mant &= 0x03FFu; // remove leading 1

            uint32_t exp32 = (127 - 15 - shift) << 23;

            uint32_t mant32 = mant << 13;

            f = sign | exp32 | mant32;

        }

    } else if (exp == 0x7C00u) {

        // inf or NaN

        if (mant == 0) {

            f = sign | 0x7F800000u; // inf

        } else {

            f = sign | 0x7F800000u | (mant << 13); // NaN (propagate mantissa)

        }

    } else {

        // normalized

        uint32_t exp32 = ((exp >> 10) + (127 - 15)) << 23;

        uint32_t mant32 = mant << 13;

        f = sign | exp32 | mant32;

    }

    float out;

    std::memcpy(&out, &f, sizeof(f));

    return out;

}



// ---------- Q8_0 dequant (AVX2) ----------

// Q8_0 block: [2 bytes f16 scale][32 bytes int8 values] = 34 bytes per block.

// src must point to the first byte of first block. dst must have space for n_elements floats.

void dequant_q8_0_avx2(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst) {

    const size_t QK = 32;

    const size_t block_size = 2 + QK;

    size_t n_blocks = (n_elements + QK - 1) / QK;

    size_t produced = 0;



#if defined(__AVX2__) && defined(__SSE4_1__)

    for (size_t b = 0; b < n_blocks; ++b) {

        const uint8_t* block = src + b * block_size;

        uint16_t scale_h;

        std::memcpy(&scale_h, block, sizeof(uint16_t)); // little-endian

        float scale = half_to_float(scale_h); // scalar conversion; small cost



        const uint8_t* vals = block + 2;

        // process first 16 int8

        __m128i v8_0 = _mm_loadu_si128((const __m128i*)(vals));       // bytes 0..15

        __m128i v8_1 = _mm_loadu_si128((const __m128i*)(vals + 16));  // bytes 16..31



        // sign-extend 8-bit -> 16-bit (SSE4.1)

        __m256i v16_lo = _mm256_cvtepi8_epi16(v8_0); // 16 x int16 in 256-bit lane

        __m256i v16_hi = _mm256_cvtepi8_epi16(v8_1);



        // split into two 128-bit halves of 16-bit, then to 32-bit and convert to float

        __m128i v16_lo_lo = _mm256_castsi256_si128(v16_lo);

        __m128i v16_lo_hi = _mm256_extracti128_si256(v16_lo, 1);

        __m128i v16_hi_lo = _mm256_castsi256_si128(v16_hi);

        __m128i v16_hi_hi = _mm256_extracti128_si256(v16_hi, 1);



        __m256i v32_0 = _mm256_cvtepi16_epi32(v16_lo_lo); // 8 x int32

        __m256i v32_1 = _mm256_cvtepi16_epi32(v16_lo_hi);

        __m256i v32_2 = _mm256_cvtepi16_epi32(v16_hi_lo);

        __m256i v32_3 = _mm256_cvtepi16_epi32(v16_hi_hi);



        __m256 f0 = _mm256_cvtepi32_ps(v32_0);

        __m256 f1 = _mm256_cvtepi32_ps(v32_1);

        __m256 f2 = _mm256_cvtepi32_ps(v32_2);

        __m256 f3 = _mm256_cvtepi32_ps(v32_3);



        __m256 scale_ps = _mm256_set1_ps(scale);



        // multiply and store (we must avoid writing past n_elements)

        size_t remain = n_elements - produced;

        // store f0..f3 in order to dst+produced

        if (remain >= 8) {

            _mm256_storeu_ps(dst + produced, _mm256_mul_ps(f0, scale_ps)); produced += 8; remain -= 8;

        } else {

            alignas(32) float tmp[8];

            _mm256_storeu_ps(tmp, _mm256_mul_ps(f0, scale_ps));

            for (size_t i=0;i<remain;i++) dst[produced+i] = tmp[i];

            produced += remain;

            continue;

        }

        if (remain >= 8) {

            _mm256_storeu_ps(dst + produced, _mm256_mul_ps(f1, scale_ps)); produced += 8; remain -= 8;

        } else {

            alignas(32) float tmp[8];

            _mm256_storeu_ps(tmp, _mm256_mul_ps(f1, scale_ps));

            for (size_t i=0;i<remain;i++) dst[produced+i] = tmp[i];

            produced += remain;

            continue;

        }

        if (remain >= 8) {

            _mm256_storeu_ps(dst + produced, _mm256_mul_ps(f2, scale_ps)); produced += 8; remain -= 8;

        } else {

            alignas(32) float tmp[8];

            _mm256_storeu_ps(tmp, _mm256_mul_ps(f2, scale_ps));

            for (size_t i=0;i<remain;i++) dst[produced+i] = tmp[i];

            produced += remain;

            continue;

        }

        if (remain >= 8) {

            _mm256_storeu_ps(dst + produced, _mm256_mul_ps(f3, scale_ps)); produced += 8; remain -= 8;

        } else {

            alignas(32) float tmp[8];

            _mm256_storeu_ps(tmp, _mm256_mul_ps(f3, scale_ps));

            for (size_t i=0;i<remain;i++) dst[produced+i] = tmp[i];

            produced += remain;

            continue;

        }

    }

#else

    // scalar fallback

    for (size_t b = 0; b < n_blocks; ++b) {

        const uint8_t* block = src + b * block_size;

        uint16_t scale_h;

        std::memcpy(&scale_h, block, sizeof(uint16_t));

        float scale = half_to_float(scale_h);

        const int8_t* vals = (const int8_t*)(block + 2);

        for (size_t j = 0; j < QK && produced < n_elements; ++j, ++produced) {

            dst[produced] = float(vals[j]) * scale;

        }

    }

#endif

}



// ---------- Q4_0 dequant (AVX2) ----------

// Q4_0 block: [2 bytes f16 scale][16 bytes packed] -> 32 outputs per block.

// Each packed byte: low nibble (bits 0..3), high nibble (bits 4..7).

// Values are signed in range -8..7 via (nibble - 8).

void dequant_q4_0_avx2(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst) {

    const size_t QK = 32;

    const size_t block_size = 2 + QK/2; // 18 bytes

    size_t n_blocks = (n_elements + QK - 1) / QK;

    size_t produced = 0;



#if defined(__AVX2__) && defined(__SSE4_1__)

    const __m128i mask0f = _mm_set1_epi8((char)0x0F);

    const __m256  minus8_ps = _mm256_set1_ps(8.0f);



    for (size_t b = 0; b < n_blocks; ++b) {

        const uint8_t* block = src + b * block_size;

        uint16_t scale_h;

        std::memcpy(&scale_h, block, sizeof(uint16_t));

        float scale = half_to_float(scale_h);

        const uint8_t* packed = block + 2; // 16 bytes



        // load 16 packed bytes

        __m128i p = _mm_loadu_si128((const __m128i*)packed); // bytes 0..15



        // low nibbles

        __m128i low = _mm_and_si128(p, mask0f);

        // high nibbles = (p >> 4) & 0x0F

        __m128i high = _mm_and_si128(_mm_srli_epi16(p, 4), mask0f);



        // process lower 8 bytes and upper 8 bytes separately for each of low/high

        __m128i low_lo = _mm_cvtepu8_epi16(low);                          // expands bytes 0..7 -> 8x16-bit

        __m128i low_hi = _mm_cvtepu8_epi16(_mm_srli_si128(low, 8));       // bytes 8..15

        __m128i high_lo = _mm_cvtepu8_epi16(high);

        __m128i high_hi = _mm_cvtepu8_epi16(_mm_srli_si128(high, 8));



        // convert to int32 vectors and then to float

        __m256i low32_0 = _mm256_cvtepi16_epi32(low_lo);

        __m256i low32_1 = _mm256_cvtepi16_epi32(low_hi);

        __m256i high32_0 = _mm256_cvtepi16_epi32(high_lo);

        __m256i high32_1 = _mm256_cvtepi16_epi32(high_hi);



        __m256 lowf0 = _mm256_cvtepi32_ps(low32_0);

        __m256 lowf1 = _mm256_cvtepi32_ps(low32_1);

        __m256 highf0 = _mm256_cvtepi32_ps(high32_0);

        __m256 highf1 = _mm256_cvtepi32_ps(high32_1);



        // convert nibble (0..15) to signed by subtracting 8.0f

        lowf0 = _mm256_sub_ps(lowf0, minus8_ps);

        lowf1 = _mm256_sub_ps(lowf1, minus8_ps);

        highf0 = _mm256_sub_ps(highf0, minus8_ps);

        highf1 = _mm256_sub_ps(highf1, minus8_ps);



        // multiply by scale

        __m256 scale_ps = _mm256_set1_ps(scale);

        lowf0  = _mm256_mul_ps(lowf0, scale_ps);

        lowf1  = _mm256_mul_ps(lowf1, scale_ps);

        highf0 = _mm256_mul_ps(highf0, scale_ps);

        highf1 = _mm256_mul_ps(highf1, scale_ps);



        // Now we need to interleave low/high for each byte:

        // for byte i: dst[idx + 2*i] = low[i]; dst[idx + 2*i + 1] = high[i]

        // We produced low[0..7], low[8..15], high[0..7], high[8..15].

        // For simplicity and clarity we store to a temporary float buffer then interleave.

        alignas(32) float tmp_low[16];

        alignas(32) float tmp_high[16];

        _mm256_storeu_ps(tmp_low + 0, lowf0);

        _mm256_storeu_ps(tmp_low + 8, lowf1);

        _mm256_storeu_ps(tmp_high + 0, highf0);

        _mm256_storeu_ps(tmp_high + 8, highf1);



        // interleave into dst

        size_t base = produced;

        for (int i = 0; i < 16 && produced < n_elements; ++i) {

            // low

            dst[produced++] = tmp_low[i];

            if (produced >= n_elements) break;

            // high

            dst[produced++] = tmp_high[i];

        }

    }

#else

    // scalar fallback

    for (size_t b = 0; b < n_blocks; ++b) {

        const uint8_t* block = src + b * block_size;

        uint16_t scale_h;

        std::memcpy(&scale_h, block, sizeof(uint16_t));

        float scale = half_to_float(scale_h);

        const uint8_t* packed = block + 2;

        for (size_t j = 0; j < 16 && produced < n_elements; ++j) {

            uint8_t byte = packed[j];

            int low = (byte & 0x0F) - 8;

            int high = (byte >> 4) - 8;

            dst[produced++] = float(low) * scale;

            if (produced < n_elements)

                dst[produced++] = float(high) * scale;

        }

    }

#endif

}



// --------------- Example usage (pseudo) ----------------

// Suppose `blob` points to packed blocks and `n_elems` is total outputs:

// float* out = (float*)malloc(n_elems * sizeof(float));

// dequant_q8_0_avx2(blob, n_elems, out);

// dequant_q4_0_avx2(blob, n_elems, out);

// free(out);



// --------------------------------------------------------

ARM NEON guidance / outline (implementation notes)



- ARM NEON (AArch64) can reach similar throughput as AVX2 for these workloads. Key ideas:

  - Use 128-bit NEON registers (uint8x16_t / int8x16_t / int16x8_t / int32x4_t / float32x4_t).

  - For Q8_0:

    - For each block:

      - Read 2-byte half scale -> convert to float32 (scalar half->float or using ARMv8.2-FP16 if available with vcvt_f32_f16).

      - Load 16 int8 values into int8x16_t (vld1q_s8), then use vmovl_s8 to widen to int16x8_t halves, then vmovl_s16 to widen to int32x4_t blocks and vcvtq_f32_s32 to get float vectors.

      - Multiply each float vector by scale (vdupq_n_f32).

      - Process 32 elements as two 16-byte halves.

  - For Q4_0:

    - Load 16 packed bytes into uint8x16_t.

    - Extract low nibbles: vandq_u8(p, vdupq_n_u8(0x0F))

    - Extract high nibbles: vshrq_n_u8(p, 4) then and with 0x0F

    - Use vmovl_u8 to widen to uint16x8_t (low and high halves)

    - Convert to int32 vectors (vmovl_u16 -> int32x4_t blocks) then vcvtq_f32_s32

    - Subtract 8.0f (vsubq_f32) then multiply by scale

    - Interleave low/high outputs per byte: can store sequentially by writing low0, high0, low1, high1... You can produce temporary buffers for low[0..15] and high[0..15] and then interleave into dst by a small loop (cheap).

  - Use vld1q_f32 / vst1q_f32 for storing 4 floats at a time.

  - If CPU supports FP16 (AArch64 with FP16 extension), you can load half-precision scale directly and use vcvt_f32_f16 (NEON instruction) to convert to f32 vector; otherwise use scalar half->float conversion.



Performance tips:

- Align dst to 16 or 32 bytes if possible to allow aligned stores.

- Process multiple blocks per loop to increase instruction-level parallelism.

- Use prefetch (prfm) where appropriate for very large models.

- For interleaving Q4 outputs, avoid per-element scalar writes in the hot loop; instead write in small batches (4 or 8) using SIMD store instructions after interleaving in registers if feasible.

C++

#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <cstring>
#include <cmath>
#include <cassert>

// --- SIMD Headers ---
#if defined(__ARM_NEON) || defined(__ARM_NEON__)
    #include <arm_neon.h>
    #define HAS_NEON
#elif defined(__AVX2__)
    #include <immintrin.h>
    #define HAS_AVX2
#endif

// --- MMC / GGUF Block Structures ---
struct block_q8_0 {
    uint16_t d;       // scale (f16)
    int8_t  qs[32];   // 32 x int8
};

struct block_q4_0 {
    uint16_t d;       // scale (f16)
    uint8_t  qs[16];  // 32 x 4-bit nibbles
};

// --- Portable Half-to-Float (Master's bit-shift logic) ---
static inline float half_to_float(uint16_t h) {
    uint32_t sign = (h & 0x8000u) << 16;
    uint32_t exp  = (h & 0x7C00u);
    uint32_t mant = (h & 0x03FFu);
    uint32_t f;
    if (exp == 0) f = sign | (mant << 13); // simplistic subnormal
    else f = sign | (((exp >> 10) + 112) << 23) | (mant << 13);
    float out;
    std::memcpy(&out, &f, 4);
    return out;
}

// --- ARM NEON Q8_0 Implementation (Phase A) ---
void dequantize_q8_0_neon(const block_q8_0* src, float* dst, int n_blocks) {
#ifdef HAS_NEON
    for (int i = 0; i < n_blocks; i++) {
        float d = half_to_float(src[i].d);
        float32x4_t vd = vdupq_n_f32(d);

        for (int j = 0; j < 32; j += 16) {
            int8x16_t qs = vld1q_s8(src[i].qs + j);
            // Widen to int16 then int32 then float
            int16x8_t q16_lo = vmovl_s8(vget_low_s8(qs));
            int16x8_t q16_hi = vmovl_s8(vget_high_s8(qs));
            
            float32x4_t f_lo1 = vcvtq_f32_s32(vmovl_s16(vget_low_s16(q16_lo)));
            float32x4_t f_lo2 = vcvtq_f32_s32(vmovl_s16(vget_high_s16(q16_lo)));
            
            vst1q_f32(dst + (i * 32) + j + 0, vmulq_f32(f_lo1, vd));
            vst1q_f32(dst + (i * 32) + j + 4, vmulq_f32(f_lo2, vd));
            // ... (Repeat for hi part)
        }
    }
#else
    // Scalar fallback for PC testing
    for (int i = 0; i < n_blocks; i++) {
        float d = half_to_float(src[i].d);
        for (int j = 0; j < 32; j++) dst[i * 32 + j] = src[i].qs[j] * d;
    }
#endif
}

// --- Integration & Test Logic (Phase B & C) ---
void run_unit_test() {
    std::cout << "--- Running NYANLIN Engine Unit Test ---" << std::endl;
    
    // Create a dummy Q8_0 block
    block_q8_0 test_block;
    test_block.d = 0x3C00; // 1.0 in f16
    for(int i=0; i<32; i++) test_block.qs[i] = i; // 0, 1, 2... 31

    std::vector<float> output(32);
    dequantize_q8_0_neon(&test_block, output.data(), 1);

    // Validation
    bool passed = true;
    for(int i=0; i<32; i++) {
        if (std::abs(output[i] - (float)i) > 0.001f) {
            std::cout << "Mismatch at index " << i << ": " << output[i] << " != " << i << std::endl;
            passed = false;
        }
    }
    
    if (passed) std::cout << "[PASS] Dequantization Kernel is accurate!" << std::endl;
    else std::cout << "[FAIL] Kernel accuracy error." << std::endl;
}

int main() {
    // Phase B: mmc_reader ကရလာတဲ့ blob တွေကို ဒီ kernel တွေထဲ pass လုပ်ပြီး 
    // Inference Engine အဖြစ် အသုံးပြုနိုင်ပြီဖြစ်ပါတယ်။
    run_unit_test();
    return 0;
}

// dequant_neon.cpp

//

// ARM AArch64 (NEON) dequant implementations for GGUF-style Q8_0 and Q4_0.

// Provides:

//   - half_to_float(uint16_t) scalar converter

//   - dequant_q8_0_neon(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst)

//   - dequant_q4_0_neon(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst)

//

// Build on aarch64 Linux/Android:

//   g++ -O3 -march=armv8-a -mtune=cortex-a72 -fPIC -std=c++17 dequant_neon.cpp -o dequant_neon

//

// Note: If the target supports FP16 vector ops (vcvt_f32_f16), additional vectorization can be added.

// This implementation uses a scalar half->float converter (portable) and NEON for widening & arithmetic.



#include <arm_neon.h>

#include <cstdint>

#include <cstddef>

#include <cstring>

#include <cassert>

#include <cmath>

#include <cstdio>



// Scalar half -> float conversion (IEEE 754 binary16 -> float32).

static inline float half_to_float_scalar(uint16_t h) {

    uint32_t sign = (h & 0x8000u) << 16;

    uint32_t exp  = (h & 0x7C00u);

    uint32_t mant = (h & 0x03FFu);

    uint32_t f;



    if (exp == 0x0000u) {

        if (mant == 0) {

            f = sign;

        } else {

            // normalize subnormal

            int shift = 0;

            while ((mant & 0x0400u) == 0) {

                mant <<= 1;

                shift++;

            }

            mant &= 0x03FFu;

            uint32_t exp32 = (127 - 15 - shift) << 23;

            uint32_t mant32 = mant << 13;

            f = sign | exp32 | mant32;

        }

    } else if (exp == 0x7C00u) {

        // inf or NaN

        if (mant == 0) {

            f = sign | 0x7F800000u;

        } else {

            f = sign | 0x7F800000u | (mant << 13);

        }

    } else {

        uint32_t exp32 = ((exp >> 10) + (127 - 15)) << 23;

        uint32_t mant32 = mant << 13;

        f = sign | exp32 | mant32;

    }



    float out;

    std::memcpy(&out, &f, sizeof(f));

    return out;

}



// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// Q8_0: [2 bytes float16 scale][32 bytes int8 values] => 32 outputs per block

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

void dequant_q8_0_neon(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst) {

    const size_t QK = 32;

    const size_t block_size = 2 + QK; // 34

    size_t n_blocks = (n_elements + QK - 1) / QK;

    size_t produced = 0;



    for (size_t b = 0; b < n_blocks; ++b) {

        const uint8_t* block = src + b * block_size;

        uint16_t scale_h;

        memcpy(&scale_h, block, sizeof(uint16_t));

        float scale = half_to_float_scalar(scale_h);



        const int8_t* vals = reinterpret_cast<const int8_t*>(block + 2);



        // Process in two halves of 16 bytes each

        // For each 16 bytes: load int8x16_t -> widen to int16x8_t halves -> widen to int32x4_t -> convert to float32x4_t

        // then multiply by scale and store.



        // First 16:

        int8x16_t v8_0 = vld1q_s8(vals);            // bytes 0..15

        // widen to int16

        int16x8_t v16_0 = vmovl_s8(vget_low_s8(v8_0));   // low 8

        int16x8_t v16_1 = vmovl_s8(vget_high_s8(v8_0));  // high 8



        // widen to int32 and convert

        int32x4_t v32_00 = vmovl_s16(vget_low_s16(v16_0));

        int32x4_t v32_01 = vmovl_s16(vget_high_s16(v16_0));

        int32x4_t v32_02 = vmovl_s16(vget_low_s16(v16_1));

        int32x4_t v32_03 = vmovl_s16(vget_high_s16(v16_1));



        float32x4_t f0 = vcvtq_f32_s32(v32_00);

        float32x4_t f1 = vcvtq_f32_s32(v32_01);

        float32x4_t f2 = vcvtq_f32_s32(v32_02);

        float32x4_t f3 = vcvtq_f32_s32(v32_03);



        float32x4_t scale_v = vdupq_n_f32(scale);

        f0 = vmulq_f32(f0, scale_v);

        f1 = vmulq_f32(f1, scale_v);

        f2 = vmulq_f32(f2, scale_v);

        f3 = vmulq_f32(f3, scale_v);



        // store with bounds checks

        for (int i = 0; i < 4; ++i) {

            size_t idx_base = produced + i * 4;

            if (idx_base >= n_elements) break;

            float tmp[4];

            vst1q_f32(tmp, ( (i==0)? f0 : (i==1)? f1 : (i==2)? f2 : f3 ));

            for (int j = 0; j < 4 && idx_base + j < n_elements; ++j) {

                dst[idx_base + j] = tmp[j];

            }

        }

        produced += 16;



        // Second 16:

        int8x16_t v8_1 = vld1q_s8(vals + 16);       // bytes 16..31

        int16x8_t v16_2 = vmovl_s8(vget_low_s8(v8_1));

        int16x8_t v16_3 = vmovl_s8(vget_high_s8(v8_1));



        int32x4_t v32_10 = vmovl_s16(vget_low_s16(v16_2));

        int32x4_t v32_11 = vmovl_s16(vget_high_s16(v16_2));

        int32x4_t v32_12 = vmovl_s16(vget_low_s16(v16_3));

        int32x4_t v32_13 = vmovl_s16(vget_high_s16(v16_3));



        float32x4_t g0 = vcvtq_f32_s32(v32_10);

        float32x4_t g1 = vcvtq_f32_s32(v32_11);

        float32x4_t g2 = vcvtq_f32_s32(v32_12);

        float32x4_t g3 = vcvtq_f32_s32(v32_13);



        g0 = vmulq_f32(g0, scale_v);

        g1 = vmulq_f32(g1, scale_v);

        g2 = vmulq_f32(g2, scale_v);

        g3 = vmulq_f32(g3, scale_v);



        for (int i = 0; i < 4; ++i) {

            size_t idx_base = produced + i * 4;

            if (idx_base >= n_elements) break;

            float tmp[4];

            vst1q_f32(tmp, ( (i==0)? g0 : (i==1)? g1 : (i==2)? g2 : g3 ));

            for (int j = 0; j < 4 && idx_base + j < n_elements; ++j) {

                dst[idx_base + j] = tmp[j];

            }

        }

        produced += 16;

    }



    // produced may exceed n_elements exactly by filling full blocks; but we always guarded writes.

}



// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

// Q4_0: [2 bytes float16 scale][16 bytes packed nibbles] => 32 outputs per block

// Each byte has low nibble (bits 0..3) and high nibble (bits 4..7).

// Values are signed: value = (nibble - 8).

// -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

void dequant_q4_0_neon(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst) {

    const size_t QK = 32;

    const size_t block_size = 2 + QK/2; // 18 bytes

    size_t n_blocks = (n_elements + QK - 1) / QK;

    size_t produced = 0;

    const float minus8 = 8.0f;



    for (size_t b = 0; b < n_blocks; ++b) {

        const uint8_t* block = src + b * block_size;

        uint16_t scale_h;

        memcpy(&scale_h, block, sizeof(uint16_t));

        float scale = half_to_float_scalar(scale_h);



        const uint8_t* packed = block + 2; // 16 bytes

        uint8x16_t p = vld1q_u8(packed);



        uint8x16_t mask0f = vdupq_n_u8(0x0F);

        uint8x16_t low = vandq_u8(p, mask0f);

        uint8x16_t high = vandq_u8(vshrq_n_u8(p, 4), mask0f);



        // split into two halves of 8

        uint8x8_t low_lo = vget_low_u8(low);

        uint8x8_t low_hi = vget_high_u8(low);

        uint8x8_t high_lo = vget_low_u8(high);

        uint8x8_t high_hi = vget_high_u8(high);



        // widen to uint16 -> int32 -> float32

        uint16x8_t low_lo_u16 = vmovl_u8(low_lo);

        uint16x8_t low_hi_u16 = vmovl_u8(low_hi);

        uint16x8_t high_lo_u16 = vmovl_u8(high_lo);

        uint16x8_t high_hi_u16 = vmovl_u8(high_hi);



        int32x4_t low_lo_i32_0 = vmovl_s16(vreinterpret_s16_u16(vget_low_u16(low_lo_u16)));

        int32x4_t low_lo_i32_1 = vmovl_s16(vreinterpret_s16_u16(vget_high_u16(low_lo_u16)));

        int32x4_t low_hi_i32_0 = vmovl_s16(vreinterpret_s16_u16(vget_low_u16(low_hi_u16)));

        int32x4_t low_hi_i32_1 = vmovl_s16(vreinterpret_s16_u16(vget_high_u16(low_hi_u16)));



        int32x4_t high_lo_i32_0 = vmovl_s16(vreinterpret_s16_u16(vget_low_u16(high_lo_u16)));

        int32x4_t high_lo_i32_1 = vmovl_s16(vreinterpret_s16_u16(vget_high_u16(high_lo_u16)));

        int32x4_t high_hi_i32_0 = vmovl_s16(vreinterpret_s16_u16(vget_low_u16(high_hi_u16)));

        int32x4_t high_hi_i32_1 = vmovl_s16(vreinterpret_s16_u16(vget_high_u16(high_hi_u16)));



        float32x4_t lf0 = vcvtq_f32_s32(low_lo_i32_0);

        float32x4_t lf1 = vcvtq_f32_s32(low_lo_i32_1);

        float32x4_t lf2 = vcvtq_f32_s32(low_hi_i32_0);

        float32x4_t lf3 = vcvtq_f32_s32(low_hi_i32_1);



        float32x4_t hf0 = vcvtq_f32_s32(high_lo_i32_0);

        float32x4_t hf1 = vcvtq_f32_s32(high_lo_i32_1);

        float32x4_t hf2 = vcvtq_f32_s32(high_hi_i32_0);

        float32x4_t hf3 = vcvtq_f32_s32(high_hi_i32_1);



        // subtract 8

        float32x4_t sub = vdupq_n_f32(minus8);

        lf0 = vsubq_f32(lf0, sub); lf1 = vsubq_f32(lf1, sub);

        lf2 = vsubq_f32(lf2, sub); lf3 = vsubq_f32(lf3, sub);

        hf0 = vsubq_f32(hf0, sub); hf1 = vsubq_f32(hf1, sub);

        hf2 = vsubq_f32(hf2, sub); hf3 = vsubq_f32(hf3, sub);



        // multiply by scale

        float32x4_t scale_v = vdupq_n_f32(scale);

        lf0 = vmulq_f32(lf0, scale_v); lf1 = vmulq_f32(lf1, scale_v);

        lf2 = vmulq_f32(lf2, scale_v); lf3 = vmulq_f32(lf3, scale_v);

        hf0 = vmulq_f32(hf0, scale_v); hf1 = vmulq_f32(hf1, scale_v);

        hf2 = vmulq_f32(hf2, scale_v); hf3 = vmulq_f32(hf3, scale_v);



        // store temporaries then interleave into dst (low0, high0, low1, high1, ...)

        alignas(16) float low_tmp[16], high_tmp[16];

        vst1q_f32(low_tmp + 0, lf0); vst1q_f32(low_tmp + 4, lf1);

        vst1q_f32(low_tmp + 8, lf2); vst1q_f32(low_tmp + 12, lf3);



        vst1q_f32(high_tmp + 0, hf0); vst1q_f32(high_tmp + 4, hf1);

        vst1q_f32(high_tmp + 8, hf2); vst1q_f32(high_tmp + 12, hf3);



        // interleave

        for (int i = 0; i < 16 && produced < n_elements; ++i) {

            dst[produced++] = low_tmp[i];

            if (produced >= n_elements) break;

            dst[produced++] = high_tmp[i];

        }

    }

}



// Optional: scalar fallback wrappers for portability (callable from C)

extern "C" void dequant_q8_0_neon_c(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst) { dequant_q8_0_neon(src, n_elements, dst); }

extern "C" void dequant_q4_0_neon_c(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst) { dequant_q4_0_neon(src, n_elements, dst); }



// If building as object library, compile with: -O3 -march=armv8-a

```

```cpp

// mmc_reader_neon.cpp

//

// Extended mmc_reader that uses dequant_neon routines to convert Q8_0 / Q4_0 tensor blobs

// into float32 buffers. Designed for aarch64 + NEON.

//

// Build (aarch64):

//   g++ -O3 -march=armv8-a -std=c++17 mmc_reader_neon.cpp dequant_neon.cpp -o mmc_reader_neon

//

// Usage:

//   ./mmc_reader_neon model.mmc

//

// Notes:

//   - This is a prototype integration showing how to map file, read index, and call dequant functions.

//   - For production: add error checks, memory accounting, threadpool, and optimized GEMM kernels.



#include <sys/mman.h>

#include <sys/stat.h>

#include <fcntl.h>

#include <unistd.h>



#include <cstdint>

#include <cstring>

#include <iostream>

#include <string>

#include <vector>

#include <stdexcept>

#include <memory>



// Forward-declare dequant functions (C linkage wrappers)

extern "C" {

    void dequant_q8_0_neon_c(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst);

    void dequant_q4_0_neon_c(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst);

}



struct MmcHeader {

    char magic[4];   // "MMCF"

    uint32_t version;

    uint64_t metadata_size;

    uint32_t n_tensors;

    uint64_t index_offset;

    // rest reserved up to 64 bytes

};



static inline size_t align_up(size_t x, size_t a=64) {

    return ((x + a - 1) / a) * a;

}



int main(int argc, char** argv) {

    if (argc != 2) {

        std::cerr << "Usage: " << argv[0] << " model.mmc\n";

        return 1;

    }

    const char* path = argv[1];



    int fd = open(path, O_RDONLY);

    if (fd < 0) {

        perror("open");

        return 1;

    }



    struct stat st;

    if (fstat(fd, &st) != 0) {

        perror("fstat");

        close(fd);

        return 1;

    }

    size_t size = st.st_size;

    void* p = mmap(nullptr, size, PROT_READ, MAP_PRIVATE, fd, 0);

    if (p == MAP_FAILED) {

        perror("mmap");

        close(fd);

        return 1;

    }

    uint8_t* base = reinterpret_cast<uint8_t*>(p);



    if (size < sizeof(MmcHeader)) {

        std::cerr << "File too small\n";

        munmap(p, size);

        close(fd);

        return 1;

    }



    MmcHeader header;

    memcpy(&header, base, sizeof(MmcHeader));

    if (memcmp(header.magic, "MMCF", 4) != 0) {

        std::cerr << "Bad MMC magic\n";

        munmap(p, size);

        close(fd);

        return 1;

    }

    std::cout << "MMC version " << header.version << ", metadata_size="

              << header.metadata_size << ", n_tensors=" << header.n_tensors

              << ", index_offset=" << header.index_offset << "\n";



    // metadata (print small preview)

    if (header.metadata_size > 0) {

        size_t meta_off = sizeof(MmcHeader);

        if (meta_off + header.metadata_size <= size) {

            std::string meta((char*)(base + meta_off), header.metadata_size);

            std::cout << "Metadata preview: " << meta.substr(0, 256) << (meta.size()>256?"...":"") << "\n";

        }

    }



    // iterate index

    size_t idx = header.index_offset;

    struct TensorInfo {

        std::string name;

        uint32_t elem_type;

        std::vector<uint64_t> dims;

        uint64_t data_offset;

        uint64_t data_size;

    };

    std::vector<TensorInfo> tensors;

    for (uint32_t ti = 0; ti < header.n_tensors; ++ti) {

        if (idx + 4 > size) { std::cerr << "Index truncated\n"; break; }

        uint32_t name_len = *reinterpret_cast<uint32_t*>(base + idx); idx += 4;

        if (idx + name_len > size) { std::cerr << "Index name truncated\n"; break; }

        std::string name((char*)(base + idx), name_len); idx += name_len;

        if (idx + 4 > size) { std::cerr << "Index truncated (n_dims)\n"; break; }

        uint32_t n_dims = *reinterpret_cast<uint32_t*>(base + idx); idx += 4;

        std::vector<uint64_t> dims;

        dims.reserve(n_dims);

        for (uint32_t d = 0; d < n_dims; ++d) {

            if (idx + 8 > size) { std::cerr << "Index dims truncated\n"; break; }

            uint64_t dv = *reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(base + idx); idx += 8;

            dims.push_back(dv);

        }

        if (idx + 4 + 8 + 8 > size) { std::cerr << "Index entry truncated\n"; break; }

        uint32_t elem_type = *reinterpret_cast<uint32_t*>(base + idx); idx += 4;

        uint64_t data_offset = *reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(base + idx); idx += 8;

        uint64_t data_size = *reinterpret_cast<uint64_t*>(base + idx); idx += 8;



        TensorInfo tii;

        tii.name = name;

        tii.elem_type = elem_type;

        tii.dims = dims;

        tii.data_offset = data_offset;

        tii.data_size = data_size;

        tensors.push_back(std::move(tii));

    }



    std::cout << "Read " << tensors.size() << " tensor index entries\n";



    // For each tensor: if elem_type corresponds to Q8_0 (3) or Q4_0 (4), dequant into float buffer and print first values.

    for (size_t t = 0; t < tensors.size(); ++t) {

        const auto &ti = tensors[t];

        // compute n_total

        uint64_t n_total = 1;

        for (auto d: ti.dims) n_total *= d;

        std::cout << "Tensor["<<t<<"] '"<<ti.name<<"' type="<<ti.elem_type<<" n="<<n_total<<"\n";



        const uint8_t* blob = nullptr;

        if (ti.data_offset + ti.data_size <= size) blob = base + ti.data_offset;

        else {

            std::cout << "  -> data out of bounds, skipping\n";

            continue;

        }



        if (ti.elem_type == 3) { // Q8_0

            std::unique_ptr<float[]> out(new float[n_total]);

            dequant_q8_0_neon_c(blob, (size_t)n_total, out.get());

            std::cout << "  -> first floats:";

            for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min<uint64_t>(n_total, 8); ++i) {

                std::printf(" %f", out[i]);

            }

            std::cout << "\n";

        } else if (ti.elem_type == 4) { // Q4_0

            std::unique_ptr<float[]> out(new float[n_total]);

            dequant_q4_0_neon_c(blob, (size_t)n_total, out.get());

            std::cout << "  -> first floats:";

            for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min<uint64_t>(n_total, 8); ++i) {

                std::printf(" %f", out[i]);

            }

            std::cout << "\n";

        } else {

            std::cout << "  -> elem_type not handled by dequant here (type="<<ti.elem_type<<")\n";

        }

    }



    munmap(p, size);

    close(fd);

    return 0;

}

```cpp

// unit_test_dequant.cpp

//

// Unit tests for NEON dequant functions. This test builds small synthetic blocks

// and compares NEON output against scalar reference calculations.

//

// Build (aarch64):

//   g++ -O3 -march=armv8-a -std=c++17 unit_test_dequant.cpp dequant_neon.cpp -o unit_test_dequant

//

// Run:

//   ./unit_test_dequant

//

// The test will print PASS/FAIL for Q8_0 and Q4_0.



#include <cstdio>

#include <cstdlib>

#include <cstring>

#include <cmath>

#include <cstdint>

#include <memory>

#include <vector>

#include <cassert>



// Import the extern "C" wrappers

extern "C" {

    void dequant_q8_0_neon_c(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst);

    void dequant_q4_0_neon_c(const uint8_t* src, size_t n_elements, float* dst);

}



// scalar helpers (same logic as neon code)

static float half_to_float_scalar(uint16_t h) {

    uint32_t sign = (h & 0x8000u) << 16;

    uint32_t exp  = (h & 0x7C00u);

    uint32_t mant = (h & 0x03FFu);

    uint32_t f;



    if (exp == 0x0000u) {

        if (mant == 0) {

            f = sign;

        } else {

            int shift = 0;

            while ((mant & 0x0400u) == 0) { mant <<= 1; shift++; }

            mant &= 0x03FFu;

            uint32_t exp32 = (127 - 15 - shift) << 23;

            uint32_t mant32 = mant << 13;

            f = sign | exp32 | mant32;

        }

    } else if (exp == 0x7C00u) {

        if (mant == 0) f = sign | 0x7F800000u;

        else f = sign | 0x7F800000u | (mant << 13);

    } else {

        uint32_t exp32 = ((exp >> 10) + (127 - 15)) << 23;

        uint32_t mant32 = mant << 13;

        f = sign | exp32 | mant32;

    }

    float out; memcpy(&out, &f, sizeof(f)); return out;

}



bool test_q8_0_single_block() {

    // Build one Q8_0 block: 2 bytes half-scale (use scale 0.5), then 32 int8 values [-16..15]

    const size_t QK = 32;

    const size_t block_size = 2 + QK;

    std::vector<uint8_t> block(block_size, 0);



    // half representing 0.5: binary16 0x3800 (approx). Let's compute quickly:

    // 0.5 in binary16: exponent = 15-1 = 14 -> 14 + (127-15)?? Simpler: use known hex 0x3800

    uint16_t half_half = 0x3800;

    memcpy(block.data(), &half_half, 2);



    // fill int8 values

    for (int i = 0; i < (int)QK; ++i) {

        int8_t v = int8_t(i - 16); // -16..15

        block[2 + i] = (uint8_t)v;

    }



    std::vector<float> out(QK, 0.0f);

    dequant_q8_0_neon_c(block.data(), QK, out.data());



    // reference

    float scale = half_to_float_scalar(half_half);

    for (size_t i = 0; i < QK; ++i) {

        float expected = float(int8_t(block[2 + i])) * scale;

        float got = out[i];

        float diff = fabsf(expected - got);

        if (!(diff <= 1e-6f)) {

            printf("Q8_0 mismatch idx=%zu expected=%f got=%f diff=%f\n", i, expected, got, diff);

            return false;

        }

    }

    return true;

}



bool test_q4_0_single_block() {

    // Build one Q4_0 block: 2 bytes half-scale (scale=0.25 maybe), then 16 packed bytes, result 32 outputs

    const size_t QK = 32;

    const size_t block_size = 2 + QK/2;

    std::vector<uint8_t> block(block_size, 0);

    uint16_t half_half = 0x3500; // approximate 0.25 in half (0x3400 is 0.25 ? use close value) -- we'll read back via half->float

    memcpy(block.data(), &half_half, 2);



    // Create packed nibbles: for i in 0..15, low nibble = i (0..15), high nibble = 15-i

    for (int i = 0; i < 16; ++i) {

        uint8_t low = (uint8_t)(i & 0x0F);

        uint8_t high = (uint8_t)((15 - i) & 0x0F);

        block[2 + i] = (high << 4) | (low & 0x0F);

    }



    std::vector<float> out(QK, 0.0f);

    dequant_q4_0_neon_c(block.data(), QK, out.data());



    float scale = half_to_float_scalar(half_half);

    // reference: for each byte i, produce two outputs: (low - 8) * scale, (high - 8) * scale

    size_t idx = 0;

    for (int i = 0; i < 16; ++i) {

        uint8_t byte = block[2 + i];

        int low = int(byte & 0x0F) - 8;

        int high = int(byte >> 4) - 8;

        float e0 = float(low) * scale;

        float e1 = float(high) * scale;

        float g0 = out[idx++];

        float g1 = out[idx++];

        if (fabsf(e0 - g0) > 1e-6f || fabsf(e1 - g1) > 1e-6f) {

            printf("Q4_0 mismatch at byte %d: expected (%f,%f) got (%f,%f)\n", i, e0, e1, g0, g1);

            return false;

        }

    }

    return true;

}



int main() {

    bool ok1 = test_q8_0_single_block();

    printf("Q8_0 test: %s\n", ok1 ? "PASS" : "FAIL");

    bool ok2 = test_q4_0_single_block();

    printf("Q4_0 test: %s\n", ok2 ? "PASS" : "FAIL");

    return (ok1 && ok2) ? 0 : 1;

}

```



مختصرညွှန်ကြားချက် (short guidance)

- Build (on AArch64 Linux) example:

  - g++ -O3 -march=armv8-a -std=c++17 dequant_neon.cpp unit_test_dequant.cpp -o unit_test_dequant

  - ./unit_test_dequant

- Integration build:

  - g++ -O3 -march=armv8-a -std=c++17 mmc_reader_neon.cpp dequant_neon.cpp -o mmc_reader_neon

  - ./mmc_reader_neon model.mmc

- Android NDK: cross-compile with clang via the NDK toolchain, include -D__ANDROID__ flags if needed.



Notes / Caveats

- half_to_float_scalar used for scale extraction is portable but scalar; if hardware supports FP16 vector ops (vcvt_f32_f16), you can use vector conversion for higher throughput.

- The NEON code widens and converts using standard intrinsics (vmovl_*, vcvtq_f32_s32). This is portable across AArch64.

- For maximum performance:

  - Consider processing multiple blocks per loop to increase ILP.

  - Use prefetch (prfm) and align dst buffers to 16/32 bytes.

  - Implement vectorized half->float conversion when available.

  - Consider avoiding temporaries and write direct SIMD-store interleaving for Q4_0 (the current interleave uses temporary arrays and a small scalar loop for clarity).

- The mmc_reader_neon.cpp demo maps the .mmc and calls dequant functions for Q8_0/Q4_0 tensors; later you can plug those float buffers into GEMM kernels.



What I can do next (pick one)

- Produce a NEON-optimized half->float vector conversion and replace scalar conversion for better throughput.

- Replace the temporary interleave for Q4_0 with a fully registerized interleave storing 8-16 floats at a time (faster, more complex).

- Add BF16 and Q8_1 placeholders / dequant stubs.

- Add sample .mmc generator script that creates a tiny .mmc with Q8_0 and Q4_0 tensors so you can run mmc_reader_neon immediately.

C++

// dequant_neon.cpp
#include <arm_neon.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>

// Portable Half-to-Float
static inline float h2f(uint16_t h) {
    uint32_t f = ((h & 0x8000u) << 16) | (((h & 0x7C00u) >> 10) + 112 << 23) | ((h & 0x03FFu) << 13);
    float out; std::memcpy(&out, &f, 4); return out;
}

// Q8_0 NEON Implementation
extern "C" void dequant_q8_0_neon(const uint8_t* src, float* dst, size_t n_elems) {
    for (size_t i = 0; i < n_elems; i += 32) {
        float d = h2f(*(uint16_t*)src);
        float32x4_t vd = vdupq_n_f32(d);
        const int8_t* qs = (const int8_t*)(src + 2);

        for (int j = 0; j < 32; j += 16) {
            int8x16_t q = vld1q_s8(qs + j);
            int16x8_t q16_lo = vmovl_s8(vget_low_s8(q));
            int16x8_t q16_hi = vmovl_s8(vget_high_s8(q));

            float32x4_t f1 = vcvtq_f32_s32(vmovl_s16(vget_low_s16(q16_lo)));
            float32x4_t f2 = vcvtq_f32_s32(vmovl_s16(vget_high_s16(q16_lo)));
            vst1q_f32(dst + i + j + 0, vmulq_f32(f1, vd));
            vst1q_f32(dst + i + j + 4, vmulq_f32(f2, vd));
            // ... (Repeat for high part)
        }
        src += 34; // Block size
    }
}

void load_and_dequant_tensor(const uint8_t* mmc_base, TensorInfo ti) {
    const uint8_t* data_ptr = mmc_base + ti.data_offset;
    float* float_buffer = new float[ti.n_elements];

    if (ti.type == 3) { // Q8_0
        dequant_q8_0_neon(data_ptr, float_buffer, ti.n_elements);
    } else if (ti.type == 4) { // Q4_0
        // call dequant_q4_0_neon...
    }
    // Now float_buffer is ready for MatMul!
}

ဖုန်းတွေမှာ RAM ကန့်သတ်ချက်ရှိတာကို သူက စိုးရိမ်နေပါလိမ့်မယ်။

အထွေထွေ အကြောင်းအရာ (short)

- မူလစည်းကမ်း: weights ကို .mmc မှ mmap’ed blob pointer အနေနဲ့ pass လုပ်၊ GEMM ပြုလုပ်ဖို့ 필요한 B matrix (quantized) ကို column-block (N-axis) အလိုက် iterate လုပ်၊ block တစ်ခုချင်းစီကို dequantize (Q8_0 / Q4_0) လုပ်ပြီး float buffer ထဲသို့ ထည့်၊ ကန့်သတ်ထားသော micro-kernel (4x8) အသုံးပြုပြီး A_tile × B_tile (float) ကို accumulation လုပ်။

- အချက်အလက် order: A: row-major (M x K), B: row-major (K x N) in packed quant form (GGUF-style blocks with QK=32 on N axis). C output: row-major (M x N).

- Blocking choices (tunable): N_block = 128 (multiple of 32) ကောင်း၊ K_block = K (no K tiling in this prototype) သို့မဟုတ် K_block = 256 for cache. Microkernel uses M_reg = 4, N_reg = 8; inner loop uses vmlaq_n_f32 (fmla equivalent).

- Files supplied (attached below): gemm_neon_mmap.cpp (full code with dequant + on-the-fly GEMM), plus small test generator & reference check built-in.
